steps.title: Understand the logic the blank must complete
steps.content:
The passage establishes: (1) the hypothesis is supported for positive charges above a certain threshold, and (2) queen honeybees exceed that threshold, which "suggests that ______". The blank must follow logically — something true of another pollinator that can be concluded from the graph. Any answer must be directly supported by the graph data and consistent with the threshold logic.
  • Choice A — 'red mason bees and paper wasps, with greater maximum charges than queen honeybees have, cannot attract the stamens.': This reverses the logic — if higher charge exceeds the threshold even more, those pollinators should attract the stamens, not fail to. Contradicts the established relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — 'paper wasps tend to repel the stamens.': The mechanism described is attraction (negative plant, positive pollinator). Nothing in the passage supports a repulsion effect for paper wasps specifically. Unsupported by the graph. ✗
  • Choice C — 'red mason bees can also attract the stamens.': If the graph shows red mason bees also carry a charge above the threshold, then by the same logic used for queen honeybees, they too can attract the stamens. This follows directly from the data and the threshold rule. ✓
  • Choice D — 'the threshold positive charge for a pollinator to attract the stamens must be greater than 200 pc.': Misstates the threshold — the passage says queen honeybees exceed the threshold, not that 200 pc is below it. This asserts a specific value not established by the passage. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The graph shows red mason bees also exceed the charge threshold, so the same logic that applies to queen honeybees extends to them. That confirms C is correct.
